mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing activity, which boosts corporate earnings and supports rising equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains negative or below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive returns despite declining INDPRO.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below a low threshold, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth, pressuring corporate margins and prompting the Federal Reserve to tighten policy, resulting in negative equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE is below 3.5%, or Rank IC between UNRATE level and returns is positive.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong GDP growth and elevated inflation signal an overheating economy, which forces the Federal Reserve into aggressive tightening that depresses equity valuations and forward returns.
candidate_trigger: both GDPC1 growth > 3% y/y and CPIAUCSL > 5% y/y
failure_condition: Over any 5-year period, SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of months when both conditions are met, or conditional mean return is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts act as a leading indicator of economic weakness during monetary tightening cycles, as rising rates reduce home construction and consumer spending, presaging lower equity returns.
failure_condition: During tightening cycles, SPX 20-day returns after three-month HOUST declines are positive in more than 50% of cases, or the signal fails to produce a negative average return.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real retail sales signals robust consumer demand, which drives corporate revenue and profit growth, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns across all regimes.
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS growth and 20-day SPX returns is non-positive over any 2-year window, or falling RSAFS coincides with positive returns more than 40% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: The first Federal Reserve rate hike after a prolonged zero-interest-rate period triggers immediate market repricing due to tighter financial conditions, causing short-term negative equity returns as investors adjust to the new policy stance.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises above 0.25% for the first time in the cycle
failure_condition: In the first 5 trading days after the rate crosses 0.25%, SPX returns are positive more than 50% of the time, or the average return is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth combined with accelerating inflation signals a wage-price spiral that erodes corporate profit margins through rising input costs, leading to negative equity returns as earnings expectations decline.
candidate_trigger: both PAYEMS monthly change > 200k and CPIAUCSL y/y > 6%
failure_condition: When both conditions hold, 20-day SPX returns are positive in more than 40% of observations, or the conditional Sharpe ratio is positive.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production reliably signal an impending economic contraction that reduces corporate earnings and triggers risk-off sentiment, producing negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If after two consecutive INDPRO declines the 60-day SPX return is positive more than 40% of the time, or if the signal fails to produce a median negative return.
